summary: STAGEPASS Next.js 14 frontend testing completed. All public pages load correctly. Navigation, auth guards, butler dock, login/signup forms all pass. 2 pages get stuck in infinite loading when Firebase is unavailable (content/[id] and c/[slug]) — a resiliency issue that needs a loading timeout. API routes work correctly.

minor.issue: Returns 'Neural link disrupted' fallback because NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_API_KEY is not configured with a real key — expected in test env but needs real key in production
Returns 401 (correct) but admin SDK (firebase-admin) needs real GCP service account credentials to function in production

ui_bugs.issue: Pages using getDocs()/getContentById() hang indefinitely in loading state when Firebase Firestore is unavailable — no timeout or error state fallback. Firebase SDK with dummy/invalid project credentials makes requests that never resolve (WebChannel retry loop). Pages show spinner forever.
public/logo.jpg is a 0-byte empty file — causes 400 errors from Next.js image optimizer. The onError handler hides it gracefully but the logo circle in nav shows as a loading/broken state.

integration_issues.flow: Firebase Firestore connection with dummy credentials
integration_issues.issue: Firebase SDK with a valid-format but fake API key (AIzaSyDummy...) and fake projectId (stagepass-dummy) initializes successfully but then hangs on getDocs()/getDoc() calls instead of failing fast. The try-catch blocks in firestore.ts don't get invoked because the promises never reject — they just hang in the Firestore WebChannel retry loop.

issues: Most interactive elements (butler button, form submit buttons, nav links) lack data-testid attributes — only NotificationBell and FollowButton have them
Homepage hero area right side is empty (no image/content) — the gradient background fills the space but the visual design could benefit from content there
Radio page station grid area is completely empty (no stations) — only heading shows. An empty state message would improve UX

passed_tests: Homepage loads — STAGEPASS brand visible, hero section with Electric Dreams Festival, navigation links
Navigation: Explore, Live, Radio links all render correctly
Explore page — category filter tabs (All, Music, DJ Sets, Film, Business, Gaming), no-content empty state
Live page — 'No live streams right now' empty state with Video icon and CTA button
Radio page — 'STAGEPASS LIVE / Global Creator Broadcast Network' renders
Login page — email/password form, Sign in button, error state on invalid login
Signup page — displayName, handle, creatorType select, email, password fields all visible
Studio auth guard — /studio redirects to /login in ~5 seconds (client-side Firebase auth check working)
Admin auth guard — /admin redirects to /login in ~5 seconds
Butler dock — visible on homepage (Sparkles icon), opens on click, shows ENCORE header, accepts message input, sends via API, shows graceful fallback
Notification bell — correctly absent when not authenticated (only renders for logged-in users)
Log in / Join buttons — visible in nav when not authenticated
Footer — STAGEPASS brand, copyright, legal links all present
/api/butler/resolve POST — returns valid JSON {text, action, emotion} (graceful offline fallback)
/api/analytics GET — returns 401 Unauthorized without auth token (correct auth guard)
failed_tests: /content/[id] — stuck in loading spinner (Firebase getDocs hanging with dummy credentials, no timeout fallback)
/c/[slug] — stuck in loading spinner (same root cause as content page)
Homepage feed — stays in 'Loading Stage...' for 12+ seconds (Firebase hanging)
action_items: IMPORTANT: Replace public/logo.jpg with actual non-empty logo image file
Add loading timeout (e.g., 10 seconds) with error fallback state to getRecentContent, getContentById, getCreatorBySlug calls — currently they hang indefinitely when Firebase is unavailable
Consider adding a Promise.race() with a timeout in firestore.ts helper functions, or use AbortController for Firestore fetch calls
Add data-testid attributes to: butler dock button, nav links, form submit buttons, feed sort dropdown, and content cards
Butler API key (NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_API_KEY) needs a real Gemini API key set in production .env
critical_code_review_comments: RESILIENCY: firestore.ts functions use try-catch but Firebase SDK getDocs()/getDoc() promises never reject when offline — they enter WebChannel retry loop. Add explicit timeout: Promise.race([firestoreCall, timeout(10000)]) pattern
MISSING FILE: public/logo.jpg is 0 bytes — this is a broken asset that needs to be replaced
NO LOADING TIMEOUT: HomePage (loading forever), ContentPage, CreatorPage all lack a maximum loading timeout — shows indefinite spinner in poor network conditions
DATA-TESTID: Only FollowButton and NotificationBell have data-testid — add to butler button, nav links, form fields for better testability

BUTLER API KEY: Butler route uses NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix for a server-side API key (NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_API_KEY) — this exposes the key in the browser bundle. Should use GOOGLE_API_KEY (without NEXT_PUBLIC_) since it's used in a server route

success_rate.frontend: 75% (12/16 features fully verified, 3 stuck due to Firebase hang, 1 minor asset issue)

seed_data_creation: Created /app/stagepass/apps/web/.env.local with dummy Firebase credentials to allow UI testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is STAGEPASS Next.js 14 at /app/stagepass/apps/web/. Start dev server: cd /app/stagepass/apps/web && nohup PORT=3001 NODE_OPTIONS='--max-old-space-size=1024' npx next dev -p 3001 > /tmp/nextjs.log 2>&1 &. .env.local with dummy Firebase credentials already exists. Main issues: (1) logo.jpg is 0-bytes needs replacement, (2) Firebase Firestore getDocs() hangs with dummy credentials causing infinite loading on homepage feed, /content/[id], /c/[slug]. With real Firebase credentials these pages would work. Auth guards (/studio, /admin) work correctly redirecting to /login.

rca_of_firebase_loading_issue.root_cause: Firebase SDK initializes with dummy API key (AIzaSyDummy...) because NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_API_KEY is set in .env.local. When getDocs/getDoc is called, SDK attempts to connect to firestore.googleapis.com for project 'stagepass-dummy'. The WebChannel (long-polling) connection attempts are retried exponentially. The try-catch in firestore.ts functions doesn't catch this because the promise never rejects — it just retries.
rca_of_firebase_loading_issue.mitigation: Wrap all Firestore calls with a timeout: Promise.race([getDocs(q), new Promise((_, reject) => setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('timeout')), 8000))]). Or check Firebase connection state before making queries.
